title: Run-panel per-view-type toggles + server-start eager boot

tests.summary: 5 passed — EAGER_BOOT_OK eager-boot-fixture (real fixture own-port fitting spawned through startOwnPortFitting, rehydrated its persisted instance from the REAL view-state dir, marker asserted), LAZY_RESTORE_OK eager-boot-fixture (toggle off: not auto-started; lazy open still restores), prefs round-trip, invalid-id rejection. Full suite 493 passed / 3 skipped.

build.summary: isolated dist dir; instrumentation + eager-boot route compiled

e2e.summary: full e2e suite 34 passed / 5 skipped on the sandboxed harness (GARRISON_DISABLE_EAGER_BOOT=1 keeps the sandbox server from spawning real fittings). Webpack dev server verified by hand: all routes 200 with instrumentation active, eager-boot runner spawn line logged.

by: lead vision pass on live :7777 screenshots
designAudit.notes: Views card matches the existing Run card language (section/h3/mono labels, EAGER ON/OFF chip, honest always-on-persistence explainer); no emoji

video.note: W-wave consolidated walkthrough (11 beats, all vision-verified, unflagged). Per-slice beats: W1 instance refs visible in pane headers (beat 9); W2 toggles/no-save/restored session (beats 1-5); W3 eager toggles + server-boot terminal (beats 1-4); W4 workspace switch/add-pane/reload/live-terminal-pane (beats 6-9).

notes: Retry 1 (integration): Next 14's webpack instrumentation compilation cannot handle node:-scheme imports ANYWHERE in the import graph — first attempt (dynamic import of eager-boot inside the runtime guard) 500'd every route (UnhandledSchemeError), second attempt (bare child_process dynamic import) failed module-not-found in the non-node compilation pass. Fix: register() spawns scripts/run-eager-boot.ts detached via tsx (boot chain fully outside webpack) with import(/* webpackIgnore: true */ "node:child_process"). Verified: scratch webpack dev server all routes 200, runner spawned. Honest asymmetry logged: eager boot starts processes for own-port fittings; for embedded (client React) views it only warms the instance index — they restore on open by design. own-port-lifecycle's status dir is hardcoded to real ~/.garrison (pre-existing; not changed this slice).
